subject: time machine: show the live build as the present — fixes the gh-pages one-deploy lag so the current version appears and is marked live (no more travelling back to the previous version thinking it's current); make the lvOS Time Machine app always-dark like the other desktop apps; and show the real build version on the BIOS logo and boot splash instead of a hardcoded 2.0

time machine: a release is all its commits, not just the tagged tip — git show <version> expands a release to its full commit list and diffstat, git tag shows per-release commit counts, and the lvOS app rows expand to the commits each deploy shipped
time machine: add 'git tag' listing every deploy as a release (with the real v2.0.x version where the commit is tagged), let git checkout accept a version, and show the version chip in the lvOS app
time machine: git checkout now accepts any commit hash from git log, not only deploy points — a non-deploy commit resolves to the deploy that shipped it (with a note saying which)

add a time machine: git checkout <hash> in the terminal and a new lvOS app travel to any past deploy of the site — a service worker serves the real old build (back to the 2020 version) from the gh-pages history via jsdelivr, with a bar to return to the present
harden two hydration-race e2e flakes (boss key, ? shortcuts) by retrying the global keypress until the handler has hydrated
serialize deploys with a concurrency group so overlapping runs can't race on the same auto-release tag
publish my gpg public key (contact page pgp line, /pgp.txt and the gpg terminal command) and add a keybase.io/laurensv6 link to the socials

the README mobile section and its 375px lvOS screenshot catch up to round 38: the toast that now clears the front app's titlebar (visible in the shot), the touch controls on the new Asteroids and Pet apps and the wrapping four-button whiteboard tools, and the screensaver's 'tap to wake' hint on a phone

the README mobile section and its 375px lvOS screenshot catch up to round 37's phone fixes: the top-anchored notification toasts that no longer cover a maximized app's controls, the museum 'walk the floor' map fitting at 320px, the thumb-size Settings colour swatches, and the un-clipped /contact input and single-line Mail date

refresh the README mobile section and its 375px lvOS screenshot for this round's phone fixes: the wrapping co-draw whiteboard tools (so the confirm-guarded 'clear' stays on-screen), the terminal quick-key row that fits all its keys at 320px, and the visible thumb-size Files delete target

the README mobile rundown and its 375px lvOS screenshot catch up to this round — the reachable theme toggle, the no-zoom lvOS text fields, the tappable Mail buttons, the fitting Calculator sci/programmer grids and the scannable 320px vCard QR

wait for the status-bar clock (a client-hydration signal) before pressing vim go-to chord keys in the e2e tests, so a pre-hydration 'g' keypress can't be dropped and land the flaky gb->/blog navigation on /about

harden the lazy terminal/palette: a client plugin restores and seeds the VFS on every page (so blog overrides, reseed and Files work without the terminal ever mounting), the sole ~/ctrl+k opener lives in the layout shim (no second handler racing the async mount), the overlay grabs input focus on mount so Escape/typing land, the hero polls for the lazily-mounted input, and the palette e2e opens via a retry helper

the task-manager e2e drives 2048 instead of snake: snake auto-advances and can die (game over) before the taskmgr check under full-suite load, dropping it from the process table; 2048 only moves on input so it stays a live process
the task-manager e2e gives the lazily-imported snake chunk up to 10s to render its first frame, so a slow full-suite run can't miss it inside the default 5s assertion window
the bios is reachable and usable on touch: the 'press DEL' hint is a tappable button, and the setup rows tap to select+cycle with a prev/next/save touch bar, since it was keyboard-only before
the boss-key e2e fires both b-keydowns in one tick so a slow ci runner's latency between keypresses can't blow the 600ms double-tap arming window into a false failure
the readme documents the mobile support with a 375px lvos screenshot and a 'works on a phone' rundown of the bottom nav, sheet terminal, thumb-playable games and fitted desktop
the destroyer scroll e2e waits for the ship to really point down before thrusting, so a slow ci runner can't under-rotate it into a false failure
the terminal mail test learns the inbox now holds ten messages
taskbar blur moves to a pseudo-element so the start menu keeps outranking windows
rm -rf joke blocks whole-site wipes (/, ~, *, /*) but allows rm -rf *.txt
typecheck the leaderboard test and validSubmission so CI deploys again
the footer shortcuts link gets its own class so strict selectors still resolve
relay: prune per-connection cooldowns on disconnect, rate-limit chat and cursor fan-out
the destroyer flies like Asteroids: rotate + thrust forward + space to fire, heavy momentum, no mouse-aiming
touch: grabbable resize handles, long-press window menu, a reachable delete, and the ship only scrolls when flown
the destroyer works on touch: drag flies, tap fires, and the hud grows an exit
the destroyer flies the whole page: edge-diving scrolls it, manual scrolling is grounded
the destroyer ship flies: wasd/arrows with thrust and inertia, tracer bullets toward the cursor
split the three giant e2e specs in half for parallelism
the battery is real now: command, tray icon and an honest boot nudge
the start menu takes stylized screenshots into the gallery
